design:
# minutes-tjg3: Transcription Coordinator and Apple-Native Backend Plan

## Why this exists

Minutes now has a much better Parakeet story than it did even a week ago. Long recordings are safer, diagnostics are better, release artifacts compile the feature in, and the desktop app no longer falls over on the kinds of real meetings that triggered the recent investigation.

That still does not mean transcription has a single owner in the product.

Right now, readiness, warmup, routing, diagnostics, transcript cleanup, and backend-specific behavior are spread across core, CLI, health, and Tauri surfaces. That makes every backend improvement more expensive than it needs to be. It also makes a future Apple-native backend feel larger and riskier than it should.

The goal of this plan is to fix the architecture first, then make the Apple-native backend a contained experiment instead of a repo-wide bet.

This follows the recommendation in `docs/designs/apple-native-transcription-coordinator-spike.md`.

## North star

Minutes should treat transcription as a product subsystem with a clear owner.

That owner should:
- know what backend is active
- know whether that backend is ready
- know how to warm it up
- own routing for meeting vs memo vs future streaming paths
- own diagnostics and benchmark snapshots
- expose one stable contract to CLI and Tauri

When that is true, experimenting with a macOS-native backend becomes a backend choice, not a product rewrite.

## What success looks like overall

Overall success for the epic means:
- product surfaces stop speaking in helper-specific terms
- backend readiness and warmup become one contract instead of several parallel ones
- diagnostics and benchmark output become stable enough to compare backends honestly
- current helper-backed Parakeet remains valuable and working
- the native macOS backend spike ends in a written keep-or-defer recommendation grounded in measurements

## Execution order

Recommended order:

1. `minutes-tjg3.1` Introduce `TranscriptionCoordinator` facade in core
2. `minutes-tjg3.2` Unify readiness, warmup, and backend status contracts
3. `minutes-tjg3.5` Add coordinator-level diagnostics and benchmark snapshots
4. `minutes-tjg3.6` Make backend and model capabilities first-class in product surfaces
5. `minutes-tjg3.7` Spike a macOS-native backend behind the coordinator

This order matters. The spike should not happen before the coordinator exists.

## Bead map

### minutes-tjg3.1
### Introduce `TranscriptionCoordinator` facade in core

Purpose:
- create the first real runtime owner for transcription

Scope:
- add a coordinator entry point in `crates/core`
- route the current helper-backed Parakeet path through it
- route transcript cleanup through coordinator-owned flow
- keep current observable behavior as stable as possible

Success:
- there is one coordinator entry point for batch transcription requests
- the coordinator can invoke at least the current helper-backed backend
- transcript cleanup is no longer stitched together in multiple caller layers
- CLI and Tauri call into coordinator contracts rather than backend-specific branches where practical

Done means:
- a maintainer can point to one runtime abstraction and say “this owns transcription now”
- current Parakeet behavior is not regressed
- tests still prove the current long-recording failure class stays fixed

### minutes-tjg3.2
### Unify readiness, warmup, and backend status contracts

Purpose:
- stop duplicating backend-state shaping across app surfaces

Scope:
- define one stable status payload for backend id, model, readiness, warm state, install metadata, and relevant diagnostics
- move Parakeet warmup/readiness logic behind that contract
- feed health, onboarding, and settings from the same source

Success:
- CLI, health, and Tauri no longer each invent their own Parakeet status language
- warmup and readiness become one concept with one schema
- adding a future backend does not require rethinking every desktop status view

Done means:
- a single status contract exists and is used across surfaces
- warmup can be triggered and reported consistently
- backend-specific setup details still exist internally but are not leaked all over the product

### minutes-tjg3.5
### Add coordinator-level diagnostics and benchmark snapshots

Purpose:
- make backend comparison honest and repeatable

Scope:
- define a stable diagnostics snapshot
- define a benchmark output that can compare helper-backed Parakeet against future native backends
- include cold vs warm timing and long-audio behavior

Success:
- maintainers can answer “is this actually faster or more stable?” without grepping ad hoc logs
- long-audio chunking behavior can be measured through one supported interface
- native spikes can be judged against the current path on speed, reliability, and power/memory behavior

Done means:
- benchmark and diagnostics output are stable enough to use in docs, issues, and regressions
- the current helper path is measurable in a way that future backends can match

### minutes-tjg3.6
### Make backend and model capabilities first-class in product surfaces

Purpose:
- make the architecture understandable to users, not just maintainers

Scope:
- improve wording and surface design in settings, onboarding, readiness, and related CLI output
- describe what the active backend can do and what setup it still needs
- avoid helper-specific wording in user-facing product copy

Success:
- the app can explain backend/model state in terms users can act on
- users are not forced to understand helper-specific implementation details
- coordinator and native backend work fit naturally into the product copy later

Done means:
- switching backends or missing setup yields clear guidance
- product language is capability-based instead of implementation-leaky

### minutes-tjg3.7
### Spike a macOS-native backend behind the coordinator

Purpose:
- learn whether the Apple-native route is genuinely worth it

Scope:
- build a contained macOS-native backend experiment behind the coordinator
- evaluate preload, warmup, chunked meeting transcription, and stable timestamped segment output
- compare against the helper-backed Parakeet path

Success:
- we get real measurements for cold and warm latency
- we understand memory and power tradeoffs
- we know whether output quality and timestamps are at least as good as the current helper path
- we leave with a keep-or-defer decision, not a vague “promising” conclusion

Done means:
- the spike has written results
- the recommendation is explicit
- the team can choose the next move without re-running discovery from scratch

## What we are not doing in this epic

Not in scope:
- a broad Swift rewrite of Minutes
- replacing all current backends immediately
- baking macOS-only assumptions into CLI or MCP product contracts
- polishing every product surface before the coordinator exists

## Suggested first move

The right first bead is `minutes-tjg3.1`.

If that bead lands well, the rest of the plan gets easier. If it lands poorly, that is a strong signal that a native backend spike would be premature.
acceptance_criteria: Done means: (1) Minutes has an open epic that explicitly owns the coordinator-first roadmap, (2) child beads cover coordinator facade, unified status and warmup, diagnostics and benchmarking, product surfacing, and a native macOS backend spike, (3) each child bead has concrete success criteria, and (4) the plan keeps current helper-backed Parakeet valuable while making a future Apple-native backend a contained experiment rather than a repo-wide rewrite.
notes: Release manager note: every implementation bead in this epic requires a findings-first adversarial review before closure. Do not close on green tests alone.
close_reason: Completed in order: built the coordinator facade, unified status and warmup, added coordinator-owned diagnostics and benchmark contracts, clarified backend capability language in product surfaces, and finished the macOS-native spike with a grounded defer recommendation backed by latency, memory, transcript stability, and privileged powermetrics data.

title: Auto-refresh meeting list when artifacts change on disk

title: Avoid monolithic Parakeet 600M GPU OOM on long direct transcriptions
description: Running the 600M Parakeet model directly on long full-length WAVs can abort in the Metal path with a failed to allocate private MTLBuffer assertion. Meeting chunk rotation hides most of this, but any fallback or non-meeting path that feeds long recordings monolithically can still fail hard. Follow up by adding a safer long-audio strategy for Parakeet, such as chunking long direct transcriptions, a CPU retry, or a clearer surfaced OOM error.
close_reason: Implemented: long direct Parakeet transcriptions now chunk before decode, avoiding monolithic 600M GPU runs that could OOM on long recordings.

title: Use VAD-driven chunk rotation for meeting transcription

The design should respect Minutes' existing capture architecture and avoid creating new alignment bugs in dual-source recordings.
acceptance_criteria: Done means meeting chunk rotation can use natural speech boundaries where appropriate without regressing dual-source alignment, transcript completeness, or stop/finalization behavior. Verification must include an adversarial review specifically looking for alignment drift, false silence, and worse UX at state transitions.
close_reason: Implemented VAD-driven chunk rotation in the meeting transcription path rather than the recorder path, so meetings can split on natural pauses without touching dual-source capture alignment or stop/finalization behavior. Added detector and timestamp-offset tests, fixed a chunk-level false-abort regression, and verified the real meeting pipeline on a synthetic speech clip with a 2s pause showing two transcribed chunks merged into one final transcript.

close_reason: Added /compare/fireflies-vs-minutes, refactored compare pages onto a shared compare component, extended the /compare hub, persisted a local Fireflies brief, and verified the site build after an adversarial pass on fit-based framing and system consistency.

title: Draft public compare page for Minutes vs Fireflies
description: Create a public /compare/fireflies-vs-minutes page with respectful fit-based framing and source-backed official Fireflies claims.

title: Run SERP research for Minutes compare queries
description: Research current search-result shape for Granola and Otter comparison/alternative queries, persist findings in local marketing docs, and revise compare pages if the ranking landscape suggests different framing.
close_reason: Captured local SERP/query-shape research for Granola and Otter comparison terms, noted ambiguity/noise in raw alternative queries, and persisted the findings in ignored marketing docs to guide future comparison titles and metadata.

title: Create compare hub for public comparison pages
description: Add a public compare hub route that introduces the fit-based comparison posture and links to available comparison pages.
close_reason: Added a public /compare hub with fit-based positioning and links to the current comparison pages; verified site build.

title: Draft public compare page for Minutes vs Otter
description: Create a public /compare/otter-vs-minutes page with respectful fit-based framing and source-backed official Otter claims.
close_reason: Added the public /compare/otter-vs-minutes page with respectful, source-backed fit-based framing and verified site build.

title: Document working prebuilt Vercel deploy flow for /site
description: The Git-connected Vercel deploy path currently fails after successful Next 16.2.3 builds because Vercel looks for .next/routes-manifest-deterministic.json. Document the working workaround in CLAUDE.md: use npx vercel@50.38.2 build --prod followed by npx vercel@50.38.2 deploy --prebuilt --yes --prod --scope evil-genius-laboratory from the repo root so project rootDirectory=site still applies.
close_reason: Completed

title: Draft public compare page for Minutes vs Granola
description: Create a first public compare page at /compare/granola-vs-minutes using the approved respectful fit-based brief and source-backed claims about Granola and Minutes.
close_reason: Drafted the public /compare/granola-vs-minutes page with source-backed, respectful fit-based framing and verified the site build. Adversarial review added explicit 'when Minutes is not the right fit' and 'how we evaluated' sections before closing.

title: Shift comparison content toward respectful fit-based framing
description: Update the Minutes comparison brief and SEO comparison plan so they avoid trashing alternatives and instead use fair, source-backed, fit-based positioning with explicit cases where competitors are the better choice.
close_reason: Updated the Granola comparison brief and the reusable comparison SEO plan to explicitly require respectful, fit-based, source-backed framing and to avoid teardown or dunk-style positioning against alternatives.

title: Reduce README duplication now that public docs surfaces exist
description: Update README so it points to /for-agents, /docs/mcp/tools, /docs/errors, and llms artifacts instead of acting as the only home for agent-facing reference material.
close_reason: Added a top-level docs/agent surfaces section to README, replaced the hand-maintained MCP inventory with links to generated public docs, and used adversarial review to catch and fix a markdown code-fence regression in the plugin section.

title: Add first generated error catalog surface
description: Create a first generated public error reference for Minutes, sourced from real user-facing error strings or stable identifiers, with HTML and markdown output plus a lightweight index page.
close_reason: Added first generated error catalog at /docs/errors with markdown mirror and generated data sourced from crates/core thiserror enums; verified site and MCP builds, and used adversarial review to confirm the catalog should stay source-backed rather than log-string-driven.

title: Add stable per-tool docs URLs to MCP surfaces
description: Add stable anchors to the generated MCP docs page and inject per-tool documentation URLs into tool-facing descriptions so docs travel with the Minutes MCP surface into client contexts.
close_reason: Added stable per-tool docs URLs to MCP tool descriptions, generated stable anchors for MCP docs HTML/Markdown references, and verified site + MCP package builds after fixing anchor slugging surfaced in adversarial review.

title: Sync /site installed deps to committed Next 16 lockfile
description: The repo already commits /site package.json and package-lock.json for next 16.2.3 / react 19.2.5, but local node_modules is stale on next 15.5.14 and react 19.2.4. Refresh the install state without disturbing unrelated repo changes and verify the site build on the committed dependency set.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix site font utility mappings
description: The landing site redesign uses Tailwind font utilities, but site/tailwind.config.ts maps font-sans and font-mono to undefined CSS vars (--font-geist-sans / --font-geist-mono) while globals.css defines --font-sans / --font-mono. font-serif is also left on Tailwind's default ui-serif stack instead of the redesign display face. Update the Tailwind fontFamily config and verify the site build.
close_reason: Completed

title: Generate llms.txt from manifest and MCP resources
description: Replace the hand-maintained site/public/llms.txt with a generated artifact sourced from manifest.json and MCP resource definitions so tool/resource counts and descriptions cannot drift.
close_reason: Implemented llms.txt generator, added /for-agents entry page, and updated manifest documentation/support URLs.

title: Add compiler validation, drift checks, and golden tests
description: Add validation around canonical skill sources and generated host outputs. Include deterministic dry-run checks, path and metadata linting, and golden tests for flagship skills so drift and host mistakes fail fast in CI instead of reaching users.
acceptance_criteria: The compiler has automated validation for source schema and generated outputs, plus golden tests for the pilot skills and a drift-check mode suitable for CI.
notes: Added compiler-side validation and snapshot coverage: tooling/skills/compiler/check.ts enforces structural/path rules, tooling/skills/compiler/golden.ts manages pilot snapshots, tooling/skills/package.json now has build/check/golden/golden:write/compile:dry/ci scripts, and initial goldens were written for minutes-brief/minutes-prep/minutes-debrief across Claude and Codex. Verification: node tooling/skills/dist/compiler/check.js => ok, node tooling/skills/dist/compiler/golden.js => ok, node tooling/skills/dist/compiler/compile.js --dry-run --host claude --host codex => clean.
close_reason: Completed: validation, golden tests, and CI-suitable drift checks now exist for the skill compiler.

title: Migrate the remaining Minutes skill set to canonical sources
description: After the flagship pilot skills are stable, migrate the rest of the Minutes skill set into canonical sources and generated host outputs. This task is about completing the architectural shift so the plugin is no longer a mix of hand-maintained and generated skill content beyond intentional host-runtime exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: All remaining Minutes skills that should be compiler-owned are migrated to canonical sources and generate clean host outputs for the supported hosts.
notes: Bulk-migrated the remaining compiler-owned skills into canonical sources under tooling/skills/sources, bringing the total canonical skill count to 18. Verified full discovery across all sources and full-host generation for Claude and Codex. Special cases handled: allowed-tools preservation for minutes-ideas, legacy shared-asset validation fallback for script/template/reference-backed skills, and Codex path rewriting away from Claude plugin roots. The compiler now writes the full phase-1 skill surface for both hosts.
close_reason: Completed: the remaining compiler-owned Minutes skills are now canonical and generate clean Claude/Codex host outputs.

title: Wire generated Claude output back into the Minutes plugin tree
description: Replace hand-maintained Claude skill markdown for the pilot set with generated output in the plugin tree while preserving host-runtime-owned files such as hooks and other bespoke plugin behavior. This is the first integration step from compiler output back into the tracked plugin surface.
acceptance_criteria: The Claude plugin uses generated skill output for the pilot skills without regressing hook behavior or plugin packaging.
notes: Satisfied by the pilot migration work: the Claude plugin tree now contains generated skill output for minutes-brief, minutes-prep, and minutes-debrief, all written from canonical sources through tooling/skills rather than hand-editing the plugin files directly. Hooks and other host-runtime files remain untouched, which matches the intended phase-1 boundary.
close_reason: Completed: the pilot Claude skills in the plugin tree are now generated from canonical sources without changing host-runtime-owned files.

title: Pilot canonical compilation for minutes-prep and minutes-debrief
description: Migrate minutes-prep and minutes-debrief into the canonical source format after minutes-brief lands. These complete the core meeting lifecycle pilot and test whether the compiler can handle interactive and cross-skill workflows, file conventions, and host-specific guidance coherently.
acceptance_criteria: minutes-prep and minutes-debrief generate correctly for Claude and Codex, and the pilot lifecycle flow remains coherent across hosts.
notes: Migrated the real minutes-prep and minutes-debrief workflows into canonical sources under tooling/skills/sources. Verified compiler write mode for both hosts: Claude now generates the tracked plugin SKILL.md files for prep and debrief, and Codex generates .agents/skills/minutes/minutes-prep/ and .agents/skills/minutes/minutes-debrief/ with SKILL.md plus agents/openai.yaml. This completes the core lifecycle pilot alongside minutes-brief.
close_reason: Completed: minutes-prep and minutes-debrief are now authored canonically and generate correctly for Claude and Codex.

title: Define canonical Minutes skill source schema and host config model
description: Define the canonical source format for Minutes skills and the typed host config model used by the compiler. Decide whether the source is markdown-with-frontmatter plus validation or another typed representation, and specify the per-host rules for Claude and Codex including metadata, path rewrites, field filtering, and output roots.
acceptance_criteria: A concrete schema and host-config design is documented well enough to implement without re-litigating format questions; Claude and Codex rules are explicit.
notes: Schema/host-config design completed in local private doc .claude/minutes-skill-schema-host-config.md. Locked decisions: canonical markdown-first source under tooling/skills/sources; typed host config model; phase-1 hosts = Claude and Codex only; Codex output root = .agents/skills/minutes/<skill>/ with SKILL.md + openai.yaml; Claude phase-1 remains generated skill markdown only, while hooks and meeting-analyst stay out of compiler scope.
close_reason: Completed: wrote the schema and host-config spec for canonical markdown-first skill sources and phase-1 Claude/Codex host outputs.

title: Pilot canonical compilation for minutes-brief
description: Migrate minutes-brief into the canonical source format and generate both Claude and Codex host outputs from it. Use this as the first proof that the compiler can preserve the quality and nuance of a flagship Minutes workflow while adapting host-specific packaging details.
acceptance_criteria: minutes-brief is authored canonically and generates usable Claude and Codex outputs without hand-fixing host-specific instructions.
notes: Migrated minutes-brief into tooling/skills/sources/minutes-brief/skill.md using the real flagship workflow content from the existing Claude skill. Verified compiler write mode for both hosts: Claude now generates .claude/plugins/minutes/skills/minutes-brief/SKILL.md with matching content, and Codex generates .agents/skills/minutes/minutes-brief/SKILL.md plus agents/openai.yaml without hand-fixing host-specific instructions.
close_reason: Completed: minutes-brief is now authored canonically and generates usable Claude and Codex outputs.

title: Scaffold Minutes skill compiler with dry-run generation
description: Create the initial tooling/skills compiler scaffold, including source discovery, host loading, rendering pipeline, and a dry-run mode that reports drift without writing. This task is about the compiler foundation, not full migration of the existing skill set.
acceptance_criteria: A compiler scaffold exists, supports at least Claude and Codex hosts in config, and can run in dry-run mode against pilot inputs.
notes: Scaffolded tooling/skills as a standalone TS package with schema.ts, hosts/{claude,codex}.ts, compiler/{compile,discover,frontmatter,render,validate}.ts, and a seeded canonical pilot source at tooling/skills/sources/minutes-brief/skill.md. Verification: tsc -p tooling/skills/tsconfig.json passes. Dry-run compiler works for both hosts and reports expected drift targets: Claude -> .claude/plugins/minutes/skills/minutes-brief/SKILL.md; Codex -> .agents/skills/minutes/minutes-brief/{SKILL.md,openai.yaml}.
close_reason: Completed: scaffolded the standalone skill compiler package and verified build plus dry-run generation for Claude and Codex pilot inputs.

title: Design Codex host output structure and metadata emission
description: Define and implement the Codex-specific output structure for compiled Minutes skills, including directory layout, metadata files such as openai.yaml if needed, and any host-specific instruction shaping. This task makes Codex a first-class host target rather than an afterthought.
acceptance_criteria: Codex output structure, metadata requirements, and install target conventions are explicit and implemented for pilot skills.
notes: Codex host output is now explicit in both implementation and private design doc .claude/minutes-codex-host-output.md. Implemented changes: metadata fields added to canonical frontmatter; Codex metadata sidecar moved to agents/openai.yaml to match local Codex skill precedent; renderer now supports display_name, short_description, default_prompt, and optional icons. Verified via dry-run compiler output: .agents/skills/minutes/minutes-brief/SKILL.md and .agents/skills/minutes/minutes-brief/agents/openai.yaml.
close_reason: Completed: codex host output structure and metadata conventions are now explicit and implemented for the pilot skill target.

title: Inventory current Minutes plugin surface and classify compiler ownership
description: Inventory every file under .claude/plugins/minutes and classify it as compiler-owned, host-runtime-owned, or shared asset. The goal is to decide which files should be generated from a canonical skill source versus remain hand-authored for host runtime behavior. Include plugin.json, skill markdown, hooks, helper scripts, templates, and the meeting-analyst agent.
acceptance_criteria: A written inventory exists with ownership classification for all current plugin files, plus a clear list of files that should remain hand-authored in the future architecture.
notes: Inventory completed in local private doc .claude/minutes-plugin-inventory.md. Summary: compiler-owned = all current skills/*/SKILL.md; host-runtime-owned = plugin manifests, hooks, hook tests, meeting-analyst agent; shared assets = helper scripts, templates, references. Recommendation: keep hooks and meeting-analyst out of phase-1 compiler scope; treat plugin.json as a hybrid wrapper where the skills block may later be generated.
close_reason: Completed: wrote local inventory doc and classified compiler-owned vs host-runtime-owned vs shared assets for the current Minutes plugin tree.

title: Build cross-host Minutes skill compiler
description: Create a canonical-source -> host-compiled skill system for Minutes so the existing meeting lifecycle skills can be authored once and emitted to multiple agent hosts. Initial targets are Claude and Codex. Strategic plan lives in the local private doc .claude/minutes-cross-host-skill-compiler-plan.md. This epic is about packaging, generation, validation, and install discipline for the skill layer; it does not include porting gstack's browser daemon or giant per-skill preamble model.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical Minutes skills can be generated for Claude and Codex from one source; generated outputs are validated; flagship skills preserve current workflow quality; install/update ergonomics improve; follow-up work for minutes-learn is clearly scoped.
close_reason: Completed: cross-host Minutes skill compiler plan is now implemented for phase 1, including canonical sources, Claude/Codex generation, validation, pilot goldens, and repo-local doctor/install flow.

title: Ambient memory: add structured intelligence for voice memos
description: Upgrade the voice memo pipeline with LLM auto-classification (person, project, topic tags) plus memo-specific intent/decision extraction. The ghost-context capture pipeline is already shipped and /minutes weekly already includes voice memos; the missing work is turning memos from searchable text into structured memory that downstream agents can reason about.
acceptance_criteria: Voice memos can emit structured tags and extracted intents/decisions; short memos have sensible guardrails; weekly synthesis and related recall surfaces can consume the enriched metadata without manual tagging.
close_reason: Voice memos now emit richer structured metadata through the existing memo summarization path: memo-specific tags, source/device tagging, and the existing intent/decision/entity extraction all survive with guardrails and tests.

title: Recall: add weekly synthesis as a first-class panel phase
description: Add a Weekly phase to the existing Recall panel so weekly synthesis renders directly in the desktop UI instead of only living as a terminal/skill workflow. The Recall panel already exists; this issue is about product/UI integration, not building Recall from scratch.
acceptance_criteria: Recall supports a Weekly entry point/phase, renders the weekly brief in-app, and keeps the lifecycle loop coherent with prep, listening, and debrief.
close_reason: Implemented the weekly Recall phase through minutes-ylql.3.4: backend weekly summary command, first-class Weekly phase/button in Recall, and in-panel weekly rendering.

title: Improve second-source labeling on short native-call clips
description: A 2026-04-08 native-call repro now safely maps the local speaker to Mat via source-backed stem attribution and leaves the second source anonymous instead of hallucinating a person name. Follow up by improving diarization/apply_speakers so short native-call clips with healthy voice+system stems label the non-local source as a stable speaker label (for example SPEAKER_1) instead of UNKNOWN when the second source is clearly present but brief.
close_reason: Adjusted stem-based speaker matching to allow a larger forward tolerance for short native-call second-source segments; verified in a fresh Minutes Dev.app repro that the local speaker stayed mapped to Mat while the second source kept a stable anonymous speaker label instead of collapsing to UNKNOWN.

title: Improve self-identification on native-call captures
description: Now that native-call attribution no longer invents person names from weak attendee inference, improve true self-identification for safe anonymous transcripts. Use evidence from stem-based diarization and source-aware labels to map the local speaker when confidence is justified, without reintroducing false names on mixed self+system clips.
close_reason: Added source-backed native-call self-identification so safe anonymous transcripts can map the local speaker to Mat without inventing identities for the other side; verified on fresh Minutes Dev.app repros that SPEAKER_0 is mapped to Mat while the second source remains anonymous instead of being mislabeled.

title: Preserved native call captures keep MOV bytes under a .wav filename
description: After queued native-call processing, preserve_audio_alongside_output currently renames the captured audio to output.with_extension("wav"). For native-call captures this can produce a .wav path that still contains MOV/container bytes, which breaks later reprocessing via the process command with 'Ill-formed WAVE file: no RIFF tag found'. Follow up by preserving the real container/extension for native call captures or transcoding explicitly before renaming.

title: Tighten deterministic speaker mapping for mixed self-plus-system native call clips
description: A 2026-04-08 native-call repro with local speech followed by system/TTS audio now processes end-to-end and logs attribution correctly. The new attribution log for ~/.minutes/native-captures/2026-04-08-125807-call.mov shows diarization_from_stems=true, raw_diarization_num_speakers=2, self_attribution returned_some=false with skipped_reason=no-stable-label, and the final speaker_map came entirely from deterministic mapping (SPEAKER_1 -> Mat, SPEAKER_0 -> Matt) on a mixed self+system clip. Follow up by tightening the deterministic 1-on-1 fallback so inferred/ambiguous attendee sets from summary participants do not produce misleading person-name mappings on native call captures. The next fix likely belongs in deterministic fallback policy / attendee inference, not apply_speakers or the single-stem self-attribution helper.

title: Surface capture backend/path in UI and artifact metadata
description: Recent dogfood runs repeatedly confused generic mic/room capture with native call capture. Add explicit capture backend metadata (for example capture_backend: cpal vs native-call) to artifacts/logs and surface the active backend in the recording UI so users and debugging sessions can immediately tell which path was exercised.

title: Instrument startup phases and investigate first-open freeze
description: Users observed a few-second freeze on desktop app startup. Add timestamped startup phase instrumentation across Rust setup and frontend init, identify the slow phase(s), and then remove or defer the blocking work. Suspects include Recall/PTTY restore, large inline frontend boot, and eager status/settings loads.

title: Theme: QA pass + polish across all surfaces (phase 6)
description:
Phase 6 of the Light/Dark/Auto theme refactor (see PLAN.md.theme-system).

Walk the full QA matrix in PLAN.md.theme-system section 9 on a real Mac with the dev app. Take screenshots in both modes for every screen and every state listed in the matrix (~30 surfaces x 3 modes = ~90 cells). Adjust palette tokens that look wrong in the wild.

Test environments:
- macOS Sequoia (15.x)
- macOS Tahoe (16.x — current stable on this machine)
- White desktop wallpaper (worst case for vibrancy bleed-through on Light)
- Black desktop wallpaper (worst case for Dark)

Scope likely small in code: a few [data-theme="light"] token tweaks based on what the screenshots reveal. Token values in PLAN section D10 are starting points, not gospel.

Also covers:
- README.md mention of the theme setting (one-line addition to features)
- CHANGELOG entry for the release that ships this
- Release-notes draft for the v0.(x+1).0 'Light mode' headline release

Acceptance: every checkbox in PLAN.md.theme-system section 3 is ticked. Mat has personally walked the QA matrix and would not be embarrassed to put this on the homepage. The bd issue's whole point is 'ship dark-only until we can do it right' — this phase is the bar.

Blocked on minutes-ov3 (Settings UI) and minutes-c6q (xterm) — though c6q can be cut from this release per Section 8 contingencies if it slips, with QA happening on main + Dark xterm.

title: Theme: appearance setting in Settings panel (phase 4)
description:
Phase 4 of the Light/Dark/Auto theme refactor (see PLAN.md.theme-system).

User-facing control. Adds an Appearance section to the Settings panel with an Auto / Light / Dark dropdown wired through the cmd_set_setting flow added in phase 2. The setter now also emits a custom theme-changed event so all open windows update immediately without restart.

Files:
- tauri/src/index.html: new .settings-section after GENERAL with the dropdown, plus load+save wiring in the existing settings init block
- tauri/src-tauri/src/commands.rs: extend the cmd_set_setting('ui', 'theme', ...) arm to call app.emit_to_all("theme-changed", resolved_theme) after saving

Section placement: top of Settings, above GENERAL. It's the first thing a user looks for when an app feels too dark/light.

Acceptance:
- Settings -> Appearance shows Auto / Light / Dark dropdown
- Switching live in Settings updates all open windows immediately
- The setting persists across restart in [ui] section of ~/.config/minutes/config.toml
- The CLI does not error reading a config file with [ui] section

Blocked on minutes-fai. The setting is meaningless without a working Light palette.

title: Theme: light palette + brand-aware tokens (phase 3)
description:
Phase 3 of the Light/Dark/Auto theme refactor (see PLAN.md.theme-system).

This is the visual one. Adds the [data-theme="light"] override block to every window's CSS, populated with the brand-aware palette in section D10 of PLAN.md.theme-system. Also adds @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) fallbacks for the case where the JS event flow ever fails.

Key palette decisions:
- Warm white background (#fcfcf9) not stark white — pulls a hint of yellow from --accent-warm so the brand still feels warm
- --accent-warm darkens to #a86f1f for section labels (the 'orange too saturated against white' fix from the bd issue)
- --state-warning darkens to #b76b15, slightly more saturated than --accent-warm so warnings still feel like warnings
- --accent (sky blue) stays #4da8d9 in dark, deepens to #3890b8 in light for white-bg contrast
- Surface tints invert from rgba(255,255,255,...) to rgba(0,0,0,...) with similar alpha
- Shadows go warm-tinted (rgba(40,30,15,...)) at lower alpha
- Brand-mark stays navy in both modes (decision D6) — the navy gradient is the logo, not a theme element. Light mode adds an outer 1px ring at rgba(0,0,0,0.08) and bumps the inset highlight so it reads as deliberate.

Files touched: tauri/src/index.html, note.html, meeting-prompt.html, dictation-overlay.html, terminal.html, palette/index.html.

Acceptance:
- Setting theme = "light" in config and restarting shows a fully Light app
- Setting theme = "auto" and toggling macOS appearance flips the app live without restart
- Every visual checkbox in PLAN.md.theme-system section 3 passes
- Walked the QA matrix in section 9 on a real Mac with both dark and white desktop wallpapers

Blocked on minutes-rr7 (plumbing must exist before the palette has anywhere to live).

title: Theme: native plumbing + data-theme attribute (phase 2)
description:
Phase 2 of the Light/Dark/Auto theme refactor (see PLAN.md.theme-system).

Adds the theme switching machinery without changing what the user sees. Theme stays hardcoded to dark for end-users; the change is invisible until phase 3 lands the Light palette.

Scope:
- New UiConfig struct in crates/core/src/config.rs with theme: ThemeMode (Auto/Light/Dark), default Auto via #[serde(default)]. Migration in load_with_migrations adds [ui] section to old configs that lack it.
- show_main_window() in tauri/src-tauri/src/main.rs reads config.ui.theme synchronously, calls .theme(Some(...)) for explicit pins or .theme(None) for Auto, and injects an initialization_script that sets <html data-theme=...> before first paint. Same treatment for show_note_window, show_terminal_window, and the meeting-prompt builder.
- Same plumbing for the dictation-overlay window builder (commands.rs:~4059) and palette window builder (commands.rs:~5421).
- New cmd_set_setting('ui', 'theme', value) match arm and cmd_get_settings field.
- <html data-theme="dark"> added to all 5 window HTMLs. :root selectors converted to :root, :root[data-theme="dark"]. Frontend listener block added to each HTML to handle tauri://theme-changed (Auto mode response).
- New custom theme-changed event for when the user setting (not system appearance) changes — emitted by the Rust setter and listened to by every window.

Acceptance:
- App launches in Dark mode with no visual change vs main
- data-theme="dark" present in DOM on every window
- Setting theme = "auto" in ~/.config/minutes/config.toml and restarting makes the app follow macOS appearance live
- Old config files (no [ui] section) load without error and migrate to theme = "auto"
- New migration test in crates/core/src/config.rs passes
- No FOUC on cold start

Blocked on minutes-8za (token extraction must land first or the Light palette has nothing to populate).

title: Theme: extract inline color literals into CSS tokens (phase 1)
description:
Phase 1 of the Light/Dark/Auto theme refactor (see PLAN.md.theme-system).

Mechanical extraction of all ~173 inline color literals in tauri/src/index.html into CSS custom properties. Net-zero visual change in dark mode.

Adds ~25-30 new tokens covering: surface tints (1-4 + elevated), borders (subtle/regular/strong), scrims (regular/strong), elevation shadows (1-4), state tints (recording/ready/warning/live), speaker confidence (high/medium/low), and accent washes (faint/regular/strong/border/ring). Also splits --orange into semantic --accent-warm + --state-warning, with --orange kept as a deprecated alias.

Acceptance:
- grep finds zero color literals in index.html outside :root blocks (brand-mark gradient stops are documented exceptions)
- Dark-mode screenshot diff against main is zero pixels for every screen state in the QA matrix
- cargo fmt + cargo clippy clean (no Rust changes in this slice)

This is the foundation. Phases 2-6 are blocked on this landing first.

See PLAN.md.theme-system section 6 phase 1 and section D1 for the full token list.

title: Add real light mode CSS support + Auto/Dark/Light theme setting
description:
Currently the app is pinned to dark via tauri::Theme::Dark in main.rs because the brand identity (vibrancy + warm orange + serif display) was built around dark and there is no light mode CSS coverage.

Scope when ready:
1. Add @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) overrides for every CSS variable in :root
2. Light variants for ~30 hardcoded rgba surface colors added across phases 3-13 (recall-panel material, footer-actions, search-box, output-notice, processing-bar, recording-bar, detail-card, etc.)
3. Light variant for the brand-mark gradient (currently navy blue radial)
4. Reconsider warm-orange section labels — too saturated against white
5. Light vibrancy material (NSVisualEffectMaterial::ContentBackground or similar)
6. Add Theme setting to Settings panel: Auto / Dark / Light dropdown
7. Wire setting to tauri::Window::set_theme + persist to config

Why deferred: Half-assed light mode is worse than no light mode. Better to ship dark-only and add light when there's dedicated design time, not as a tail-end of a dark polish pass.

Current pin lives at: tauri/src-tauri/src/main.rs in show_main_window() — .theme(Some(tauri::Theme::Dark)) on the WebviewWindowBuilder.
notes:
PLANNED 2026-04-07. Full implementation plan in PLAN.md.theme-system. Broken into 6 phases as child issues:

- minutes-8za: Phase 1 — token extraction (no visual change)
- minutes-rr7: Phase 2 — native plumbing + data-theme attribute (no visual change)
- minutes-fai: Phase 3 — light palette + brand-aware tokens (the visual one)
- minutes-ov3: Phase 4 — appearance setting in Settings panel
- minutes-c6q: Phase 5 — light xterm theme variant (P3, can defer)
- minutes-4nq: Phase 6 — QA pass + polish

Critical path: 8za → rr7 → fai → ov3 → 4nq. Phase 5 (c6q) is parallelizable after 3 lands and is the only contingency that can be cut from the release per PLAN section 8.

Inventory frozen on fix/call-capture-hardening: ~173 inline color literals in tauri/src/index.html outside :root, 5 secondary window HTMLs each with their own :root or none, vibrancy material currently HudWindow (not Sidebar — was changed recently). Net-new: theme-change event flow, Settings dropdown, UiConfig section, Light palette in 5 HTMLs, xterm Light variant.

10/10 bar defined in PLAN.md.theme-system section 1 + section 3 acceptance criteria. Open questions for review (D6 brand-mark, D9 vibrancy material, Q4 upgrader default) flagged in section 11 — recommend running plan-design-review, plan-eng-review, and plan-ceo-review on the plan doc before phase 1 starts.

comments.author: silverstein
comments.text:
PARKED 2026-04-08 after /autoplan dual-voice review (Codex CLI + Claude subagent across CEO/Design/Eng/DX phases, 8 voice runs total).

VERDICT: 0/25 dimensions positive consensus. 8 critical bugs in plan as written. Both models recommend PARK or DESCOPE.

Critical bugs in PLAN.md.theme-system.parked:
- C1: app.emit_to_all is invented; real Tauri v2 API is app.emit() — Phase 4 won't compile
- C2: initialization_script runs before documentElement at WKWebView atDocumentStart — null-pointer; FOUC story breaks
- C3: tauri/src/theme.css promised in Definition of Done but never created in any phase's file-touch list
- C4: Config has 22 fields, plan says 21
- C5: Phase 2 default = 'dark' contradicts Q3/Q4 default = 'auto'
- C6: 'Zero pixel diff' Phase 1 acceptance with no Playwright/tauri-driver/screenshot harness in repo
- C7: DESIGN.md left saying 'Dark mode only' after Phase 3 ships
- C8: Section 1 hero use case (projector) self-defeats D9 (vibrancy halo accepted)

Plus 10 cross-phase themes flagged independently by 2+ phases:
T1. theme.css contradiction (6/8 voices)
T2. Projector hero vs vibrancy halo (4/8)
T3. Screenshot diff acceptance unenforceable (4/8)
T4. Linux/Windows non-goal will age within 30 days (cpal PipeWire) (4/8)
T5. Settings .about-card re-theme unresolved (4/8)
T6. Q4 upgrader default = 'auto' repeats trust-eroding silent behavior change (3/8)
T7. Two theme-change event paths race with no version stamp (2/8)
T8. Config corruption + Settings save = silent data loss (2/8)
T9. app.emit_to_all is invented API (2/8)
T10. DESIGN.md says 'Dark mode only' — plan never updates it (2/8)

Trigger conditions for unparking (any one is sufficient):
1. Linux/PipeWire ships AND notarization ships (strategic catch-up window opens)
2. Real user files an issue requesting light mode (not hypothetical)
3. External commitment to ship light mode by a specific date
4. v1.0 launch where 'tool with taste' positioning needs accessibility floor

When unparking, REWRITE the plan addressing all 8 critical bugs and 10 cross-phase themes BEFORE any phase begins. Do not implement the plan as currently written. Plan file preserved at PLAN.md.theme-system.parked with full autoplan review summary baked in.

Strategic context at time of parking: Mid-conversation with Mullenweg/Automattic, OSS Capital, Lumin (Max Ferguson), Dieter (Grafana). Audience cares about Linux + notarization + premium dark feel, not light mode catch-up. Dark mode just got polished from 7.1 → 9.3 across 16 design phases — shipping that polish IS the win.

title: Unify demo corpus: one source for CLI demo_data.rs and MCP fixtures
description:
Codex adversarial review of minutes-4j8v (2026-04-20) flagged that the repo has two parallel demo systems:

1. crates/cli/src/demo_data.rs: Rust implementation with tagged and versioned demo corpus seeding, used by the CLI for generating sample data. Uses a DEMO_TAG marker so demo files can be safely identified and cleaned up.

2. crates/mcp/fixtures/demo/*.md: 5 hand-written fixture markdown files shipped in the minutes-mcp npm package for the npx minutes-mcp --demo flow.

These are separate hand-maintained corpora today. Drift risk is real: when we update demo questions, add v3 schema fields, or change naming conventions, we will forget to update both. The CLI demo already has the safer pattern (tagging, cleanup) that the MCP demo lacks.

Unification options:

Option A: Canonical source at repo root.
- Create fixtures/demo-corpus/ at the repo root.
- Both crates/cli/src/demo_data.rs and crates/mcp (via build-time copy into fixtures/demo/ or direct reference) consume it.
- Requires minor build-script glue so the MCP npm package picks up the files.

Option B: Generate MCP fixtures from demo_data.rs at build time.
- Rust build step emits markdown into crates/mcp/fixtures/demo/.
- Single source of truth in Rust.
- Slightly heavier build pipeline.

Option C: Shell out to minutes demo-seed from the MCP --demo flow.
- Defeats the no-CLI-required promise. Rejected.

Recommendation: Option A. Repo-root fixtures/demo-corpus/, both crates consume directly.

Out of scope:
- Changing the current 5 demo fixtures. Content is fine; the question is where they live.

Acceptance:
- Single source-of-truth directory for the demo corpus.
- Both the CLI demo path and the MCP --demo path read from it.
- Cleanup criterion: demonstrate the two systems stay in sync across a schema change (e.g., a v3 field addition touched only in one place).

Why this matters:
- Prevents silent drift.
- Makes schema evolution safer (change one place, both demos update).
- Reduces maintenance overhead for a solo maintainer.
acceptance_criteria: One source of truth for demo corpus. Both CLI and MCP consume it. Demonstrated sync across a schema change.

title: Relationship move: reach out to Conor Brennan-Burke (Hyperspell) about consuming Minutes as audio sensor
description:
Context: Conor's article at x.com/contextconor/status/2045957951278739520 is the clearest public statement of the context-graph thesis. Hyperspell is YC F25, cloud-first, SDK-first, and explicitly doesn't have an audio capture story. Minutes is the audio sensor piece they'd have to build from scratch.

Goal: convert a theoretical competitor into a distribution / integration channel. Low risk, bounded time investment, asymmetric upside.

Ask: can Hyperspell ingest Minutes' markdown output as a source of record for meeting-derived context? Offer to ship a reference adapter. Position Minutes as 'the audio layer your context graph reads from.'

title: Codex Round 1 minor findings batch
description:

1. docs/PRE-RELEASE-CHECKLIST.md:184 and :247 still assume homepage contains versioned aarch64 DMG URL. After 46003b0 (bpi), the site uses /download/apple-silicon.html — the verification step in the release checklist is stale.

2. crates/cli/src/main.rs:1339 and tauri/src-tauri/src/commands.rs:1795 have comments saying background processing falls back to events_overlapping_now(). After 0ddba41, that's no longer true — calendar matching uses events_overlapping(recording_date). Misleading for the next person debugging calendar matching.

3. caafaf0 (r4mk updater) pulls in a second reqwest major version (0.12) while the tree already uses 0.13. Not a bug, but avoidable dependency bloat for a UI-only feature. Consider consolidating to 0.13 everywhere.

Small batch — hit with one follow-up PR when convenient.

title: Clean up stale parakeet sidecar sockets at startup
description: Adversarial review Round 2 (2026-04-14): soc0's parakeet sidecar creates Unix sockets at ~/.minutes/tmp/parakeet-sidecar-{pid}-{model}.sock. When the parent process exits abnormally (Ctrl-C, panic, SIGKILL — see minutes-fh8o), stale socket files accumulate. Different PIDs prevent collisions, but the tmp dir grows unboundedly. Fix: add a startup sweep that removes parakeet-sidecar-*.sock files whose PID is no longer alive. Compounds with fh8o (Drop/signal handler) — ideally address both together. Reference: crates/core/src/parakeet_sidecar.rs build_launch_spec
close_reason: Completed via fix-sc batch. Stale parakeet sidecar sockets cleaned up at startup. Landed on main as e4bea0e on 2026-04-14.

title: Xbez/lmhf/r4mk polish: spurious error logs, test coverage, UX edge cases
description:
Adversarial review 2026-04-14 surfaced several smaller issues worth a batched polish pass:

1. xbez (73387e7): maybe_refine_title_with_llm runs even when summarization.engine=="none". Inner refine_title short-circuits but emits outcome=error telemetry on every processed meeting. Add an early return when engine is none.

2. xbez: llm_title_refinement_failure_falls_back_to_algorithmic_title test verifies outcome=error but does not exercise markdown::rename_meeting in the fallback path. Add a test that starts with Untitled Recording, forces LLM error, asserts file is renamed to non-Untitled algorithmic result.

3. soc0 (1004991): transcribe_via_global_sidecar falls back to subprocess on error and logs warn but does not emit logging::log_step JSON with fallback reason. Breaks observability parity with lmhf's pipeline logging.

4. r4mk (caafaf0): tauri/src/index.html:406-407 shows cancel for Checking phase but verify cmd_cancel_update_install is safe to invoke pre-download. If unsafe, either disable cancel during Checking or make the cancel path safe.

5. r4mk: tauri/src-tauri/src/main.rs:135 uses app.config().identifier.contains(".dev") - substring match. Use ends_with(".dev") to avoid matching hypothetical .developer.

6. bpi (46003b0): orphaned APPLE_SILICON_DMG export in site/lib/release.ts now that the dynamic HTML page handles redirection. Remove from generator if regenerated on release.

7. q3ro (a0c9785): "~35% faster" claim is N=1 sample. Add sample size + caveat to docs/designs/parakeet-perf-2026-04-14.md.

8. 0ddba41: dedup_events doc comment at calendar.rs:347 says "keeping the one closest to now" but now it's relative to recording_date, not wall clock. Update comment.

title: Tune graph-derived Parakeet boost defaults before enabling globally
description: minutes-fjxj adds graph-derived Parakeet boost phrases, but the first evaluation was mixed: a graph-derived Wintermute phrase improved capitalization while a degraded CosaNostra phrase regressed from Cosa to Coza under boost. Revisit the phrase source, score, and default-on decision with broader real-world samples before enabling graph boosts by default.
close_reason: Completed: doc-only decision recorded in docs/designs/parakeet-boost-tuning-2026-04-14.md (landed on main as 142dd09). Keep parakeet_boost_limit=0 and parakeet_boost_score=2.0 until phrase selection filtering is addressed. Phrase-quality follow-up filed as minutes-0wl2.

title: Honor XDG_CONFIG_HOME in Config::config_path
description: The CLI/watcher benchmarking harness showed that Config::config_path() prefers HOME/.config/minutes/config.toml and ignores XDG_CONFIG_HOME overrides, which makes temporary per-run configs hard to use. Respect XDG_CONFIG_HOME when present before falling back to HOME/.config and native platform config paths.
close_reason: Completed: XDG_CONFIG_HOME support landed on main as 4d7c336 + post-merge fmt fix ec0ff16 on 2026-04-14. Config::config_path() now prefers XDG_CONFIG_HOME/minutes/config.toml when XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set, falls back to ~/.config/minutes/config.toml otherwise. Migration note: legacy macOS ~/Library/Application Support/minutes/config.toml fallback was intentionally dropped (Option A from review).

acceptance_criteria: Done means: (1) a maintainer runs powermetrics or an equally trustworthy privileged energy tool on Apple Silicon, (2) helper-backed Parakeet energy data is captured for at least one speech-heavy sample, (3) the method is documented so future backend spikes can reuse it, and (4) the results are linked back into the Apple-native backend decision record.

title: Spike an Apple-native in-process transcription coordinator

It is a design and measurement exercise to determine whether an Apple-native path is worth the complexity, what the runtime contract should look like, and which pieces of the current helper-based Parakeet path would still remain useful.
acceptance_criteria: Done means the Apple-native coordinator spike ends with a concrete recommendation backed by code-level exploration or measurement: what a native coordinator would own, what interfaces it would expose, what it would replace, and whether the complexity is justified for Minutes. Verification must include an adversarial review against architecture fantasy, hidden platform lock-in, and undercounted maintenance cost.
notes: Created docs/designs/apple-native-transcription-coordinator-spike.md with a concrete recommendation: build a coordinator facade over the current helper-backed backend first, then evaluate a native Apple backend behind that contract. Included current benchmark data, explicit ownership boundaries, replacement boundaries, and phased plan.
close_reason: Completed the coordinator spike with a concrete design memo in docs/designs/apple-native-transcription-coordinator-spike.md. Recommendation: build an in-process TranscriptionCoordinator facade over the current helper-backed backend first, then evaluate a native Apple backend behind that contract. Included current benchmark data, explicit ownership and replacement boundaries, phased plan, and an adversarial review against platform lock-in and architecture fantasy.

title: Theme: light xterm theme variant (phase 5)
description:

- tauri/src/vendor/xterm/minutes-theme.js: add window.MINUTES_XTERM_THEME_LIGHT with all 22 color tokens; add window.minutesXtermTheme(mode) picker
